task_completion.description: Create a Hot Water Loop named 'Perimeter_HW_Loop' with the correct type and temperature.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an eQUEST Water Loop Properties dialog or spreadsheet view showing a loop named 'Perimeter_HW_Loop' with Type set to 'Pipe', Subtype set to 'Hot Water', and Design Supply Temp set to '180'.

task_completion.description: Create a Boiler named 'Perimeter_Boiler' with the correct Heat Input Ratio.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an eQUEST Boiler Properties dialog or spreadsheet view showing a boiler named 'Perimeter_Boiler' with the Heat Input Ratio (HIR) field set to '1.04'.

task_completion.description: Configure the four top floor perimeter zones to use Hot Water baseboard heating linked to the new loop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for eQUEST Zone Properties dialogs or spreadsheet views for zones 'T.S31', 'T.E32', 'T.N33', and 'T.W34'. The Baseboard source/type must be set to 'Hot Water' and the assigned Loop must be 'Perimeter_HW_Loop'.

task_completion.description: Set the baseboard capacity and control type for the four perimeter zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for eQUEST Zone Properties dialogs or spreadsheet views for the four target zones showing the Baseboard Capacity set to '15000' (BTU/hr) and the Control type set to 'Thermostatic'.
Look for the eQUEST simulation progress dialog box, a 'Simulation Complete' message, or the viewing of simulation output reports.
Look for the agent clicking 'File -> Save', using the save icon, or the absence of an unsaved changes indicator (like an asterisk) in the eQUEST window title bar after modifications.

integrity.description: The agent must use the eQUEST graphical user interface to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent is interacting with eQUEST dialog boxes, menus, and spreadsheet views rather than just opening the .inp file in a text editor (like Notepad or Vim) and typing the parameters.

integrity.description: The simulation must be executed within the eQUEST application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the simulation progress bar or completion dialog is a genuine eQUEST window and not a mocked terminal output or script.

integrity.description: The agent should not use external scripts to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no terminal windows showing python or bash scripts being executed to directly modify the 4StoreyBuilding.inp file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target building model is '4StoreyBuilding'. The four target zones are verified as 'T.S31', 'T.E32', 'T.N33', and 'T.W34'. The hot water loop design supply temperature must be exactly 180°F. The boiler Heat Input Ratio (HIR) must be exactly 1.04. The baseboard capacity for each of the four zones must be exactly 15000 BTU/hr. Running the simulation is a strict requirement for task success.
